paper_title: Analysis Tools and Diversity Model of Pliocene-Pleistocene Nannofossil of Kendeng Zone

methods.description: Process for conducting field mapping and collecting rock samples for nannofossil analysis.

steps.description: Field mapping and representative sampling conducted during the field mapping.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field mapping and collect representative rock samples

steps.description: Taking rock samples consisting of fine-grained rock (marl, shale) and carbonaceous.
steps.searchable_summary: collect rock samples of marl, shale, and carbonaceous

methods.description: Preparation of rock samples using smear slide method and identification of nannofossils using light microscopy.

steps.description: Sample preparation for the fossils uses smear slide method.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare smear slides from rock samples

steps.description: Nannofossils were determined under two light microscope techniques (parallel light and crossed nicols) at magnification of 1000x.
steps.searchable_summary: identify nannofossils using light microscopy at 1000x magnification

steps.description: Species was identified using standard taxonomy as described by Gartner, 1981, Perch-Nielsen, and the Nannotax3 website.
steps.searchable_summary: identify nannofossil species using standard taxonomy

methods.description: Quantitative analysis of nannofossil species diversity using Shannon-Weiner and Simpson Index.

steps.description: Calculate the diversity of nannofossils using Shannon Index Diversity and Simpson Index.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Shannon and Simpson diversity indices for nannofossils

steps.description: Shannon Index assumes all species are represented in samples and samples randomly.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Shannon Index assumptions to diversity calculation

steps.description: Simpson Index is the dominance index because it gives more weight to the common or dominant species.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Simpson Index to emphasize dominant species in diversity calculation
